item.title: The Fierce Battle Over the True Height of Mount Rainier
item.dc:creator: Peter Frick-Wright

item.title: On the Camino de Santiago, José Andrés Recovers from the Weight of the World
item.dc:creator: Kevin Sintumuang

item.title: She’s Funding Her 2,600-Mile PCT Hike with OnlyFans. Traditionalists Are Skeptical. She Couldn’t Care Less.
item.dc:creator: Grayson Haver Currin

item.title: Two Texas Flood Victims Still Haven’t Been Found. These People Won’t Stop Looking.
item.dc:creator: Ian Dille

item.title: I Spent a Week in the Jungle to Find Out Why Women Are Paying Thousands to Talk About Hormones
item.description: Could a week in the jungle rewrite the rules of menopause?
item.dc:creator: Christine Yu

item.title: Want to Climb the World’s Highest Peak? Mount Everest Training Is Anything But Easy.
item.description: How do climbers train their bodies to reach the summit of Everest? There’s lots of jogging, a few laps on a homemade ladder bridge, and maybe even a frigid climb in the middle of the night, for starters. These days, Everest coaches and training plans prepare a climber’s legs, lungs, and self-confidence for the rigors of the peak.
item.dc:creator: Frederick Dreier

item.title: HBO's New Series on the Life and Death of Climber Dean Potter Is Emotional Dynamite
item.description: Dean Potter was the "Dark Wizard" of the Valley, a man who conjured impossible feats from a well of deep personal torment. Eleven years after he died in a wingsuit flying accident, a new HBO series dives into the man behind the magic.
item.dc:creator: Tim Neville

item.title: Bears Keep Attacking People in Japan. We Traveled There to Find Out Why.
item.description: We traveled to the front lines of Japan’s battle against the moon bear, which experts say is responsible for hundreds of attacks on humans and more than a dozen deaths.
item.dc:creator: Owen Clarke

item.title: Did His Obsession with Dark Shamans Kill Him? I Traced His Footsteps to Find Out.
item.description: A renowned researcher died after becoming obsessed with dark shamans. I traveled to Guyana to see if the stories were true.
item.dc:creator: Frank Bures

item.title: Forget Your Permit? These 10 Epic Adventures Don’t Require Reservations.
item.description: Did you miss the permit window for Angel’s Landing, Half Dome, or the Teton Crest Trail? We have 10 alternatives to keep your next outing epic, even if you didn’t nab a reservation.
item.dc:creator: Madison Chapman

item.title: At a Tree-Climbing Competition, the Most Important Rule Is Don't Look Down
item.description: Arborists work in a world of extreme heights and life-or-death consequences every day. I went behind the scenes of a professional tree-climbing competition to meet the people who maintain the canopies above our heads.
item.dc:creator: Calin Van Paris

item.title: The 10 Public Lands We’re on the Verge of Losing
item.description: From the most iconic national park to the newest national monument, much of America’s 640 million acres of public land is under siege. Here are the most endangered places, what we stand to lose, and our best chances for saving them.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Pearson

item.title: Why Millions of People Are Now Traveling to Find the World’s Last Dark Skies
item.description: From stargazing to aurora hunting, travelers are chasing an increasingly rare resource: natural night.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Vermillion

item.title: Charlize Theron: Barefoot Climbing, a Broken Toe, and Trying Not to Die in ‘Apex’
item.description: From ‘Mad Max’ to ‘Atomic Blonde,’ Oscar winner Charlize Theron has built a legacy on punishing physicality. In her new survival thriller ‘Apex,’ she ups the ante as a climber hunted by a human predator, ditching stunt doubles to scale rock barefoot. She opens up about raw feet, the thin line between brave and reckless, and why misery makes for the best movies.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Wise

item.title: Heli-Skiing in California Seemed Like a Pipe Dream. Then These Two Guys Showed Up.
item.description: Two ski industry mavericks believe they’ve discovered the secret of operating a heli-skiing business in the Golden State, where red tape, finicky snowfall, and plenty of danger have torpedoed past operations
item.dc:creator: Brent Rose

item.title: How I Battled the Sizing Gap in Ski Gear—Even After Losing 100 Pounds
item.description: I realized that gear isn’t just gear. It’s a gatekeeper. And gear that hugs and tugs in all the wrong places doesn’t whisper. It declares: this ain’t for you, girl.
item.dc:creator: Marisa McMillan

item.title: ‘Be Intentional with Risk’ and Other Hard-Earned Advice from Alex Honnold
item.description: Honnold talks ‘Skyscraper Live,’ his relationship with the core climbing community, and how he assesses risk and danger as a 40-year-old father of two
item.dc:creator: Frederick Dreier

item.title: At Minnesota’s Coldest Ice Fishing Derby, You Don’t Want to Fall in
item.description: Outdoor recreation in Minnesota isn’t for the faint of heart. We spent a day at an ice fishing competition in the Land of 10,000 Lakes, where Mother Nature calls the shots, the fabric of community is thicker than denim, and tenacity is everything.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Pearson

item.title: What I Learned After Spending 14 Hours at a Suburban Ice Rink
item.description: A full day inside a suburban rink revealed how one place can hold ambition, grief, faith, and escape—sometimes all at once.
item.dc:creator: Blair Braverman

item.title: 200 Degrees and Sweaty Strangers. Welcome to Sauna Fest.
item.description: An honest account of my questionable journey into the hot-and-cold chaos of the Seattle Sauna Festival.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Arvesen
